sourceMovie.title: Don't Look Up
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Don't Look Up works as a comedy movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The comedy and science fiction blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Cloud Atlas
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Don't Look Up clicked for you, Cloud Atlas (2012) is a strong next stop: both trade in science fiction and drama, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Mars Attacks!
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Don't Look Up clicked for you, Mars Attacks! (1996) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and science fiction, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Don't Look Up?
faq.answer: Try Cloud Atlas, Mars Attacks!, Finch—each shares DNA with Don't Look Up while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Don't Look Up?
faq.answer: Don't Look Up (2021) is best described as Comedy, Science Fiction, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Don't Look Up worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.1/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this comedy lane.
